tags.description: Free-form tags for filtering and discovery (e.g. ["finance", "rag", "internal"]).

provider.description: LLM provider. Common values: "openai", "anthropic", "groq", "google", "mistral", "azure", "bedrock". Determines the API client and base URL used.

id.description: Exact model ID as accepted by the provider API (e.g. "gpt-4o", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "llama-3.1-70b-versatile"). Check provider docs for valid IDs.

apiKey.description: Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0 = deterministic/focused, 1 = balanced, 2 = highly creative. Most providers recommend 0–1 for production agents.

maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens in the model response. Controls cost and latency. Check model context window limits.

topP.description: Nucleus sampling threshold. The model samples from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds topP. Alternative to temperature — do not set both.

frequencyPenalty.description: Penalises tokens that have already appeared frequently in the output (-2.0–2.0). Positive values reduce repetition.

presencePenalty.description: Penalises tokens that appear at all in the output so far (-2.0–2.0). Positive values encourage the model to introduce new topics.

provider.description: LLM provider for the fallback model. Common values: "openai", "anthropic", "groq", "google", "mistral", "azure", "bedrock".

id.description: Model ID for the fallback (e.g. "gpt-4o-mini", "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"). Must be a valid ID for the declared provider.

apiKey.description: Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

triggerOn.description: Conditions that trigger failover to this model. Omit to use the fallback for all errors.

maxRetries.description: Number of retry attempts before failing over. Defaults to 0 (immediate failover).

maxMonthlyUSD.description: Hard monthly spend cap in USD. Requests are rejected once this limit is reached.

alertAtUSD.description: Send an alert when cumulative monthly spend crosses this USD threshold. Must be less than maxMonthlyUSD.

system.description: System prompt. Use "$file:prompts/system.md" to load from a file (recommended for long prompts), or provide an inline string. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

fallback.description: Inline fallback system prompt used if the primary system prompt file cannot be loaded. Only applies when "system" is a $file: reference.

hotReload.description: When true, the system prompt file is re-read on every request instead of being cached at startup. Useful for rapid iteration without restarts.

name.description: Variable name used in the prompt template (e.g. "user_name" for {{user_name}}).

value.description: Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

variables.description: Template variables injected into the system prompt at runtime. Values support reference syntax for secrets and env vars.

type.description: "function" = local code module, "mcp" = registered via an MCP server in spec.mcp, "builtin" = provided by the runtime (e.g. code-interpreter, web-search).

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does and when the agent should use it. This text is included in the tool-call prompt.

module.description: Path to the module that implements this tool. Use "$file:tools/my_tool.py" syntax. Only required for type "function".

function.description: Name of the callable function exported from the module (e.g. "search", "run_query"). Only required for type "function".

readOnlyHint.description: MCP hint: tool does not modify state. Allows clients to show a read-only badge and skip confirmation prompts.

destructiveHint.description: MCP hint: tool may perform irreversible actions (delete, overwrite). Triggers human-in-the-loop approval when humanInTheLoop.approvalRequired includes "before-destructive-tool".

idempotentHint.description: MCP hint: calling the tool multiple times with the same inputs produces the same result. Allows safe retries.

openWorldHint.description: MCP hint: tool interacts with systems outside the agent's controlled environment (e.g. the internet). Affects trust level in tool outputs.

transport.description: "stdio" = spawn a local process (requires "command"), "sse" = Server-Sent Events over HTTP, "http" = streamable HTTP (requires "url").

command.description: Shell command to spawn the MCP server process. Required for transport "stdio" (e.g. "npx", "python").

args.description: Arguments passed to the command (e.g. ["-m", "my_mcp_server"]). Only used for transport "stdio".

url.description: Base URL of the remote MCP server. Required for transport "sse" or "http". Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

env.description: Environment variables injected into the MCP server process. Values support reference syntax. Use "$env:API_KEY" to forward secrets without hardcoding.

timeoutSeconds.description: Seconds to wait for the MCP server to respond during health checks. Increase for slow-starting processes.

maxTurns.description: Maximum number of conversation turns (user + assistant message pairs) to retain in context. Older turns are evicted first. Use with maxTokens for dual-bounded windows.

maxTokens.description: Maximum total tokens across all retained messages. When exceeded, oldest turns are evicted. Required by MEM-05 compliance rule.

ttlSeconds.description: Time-to-live in seconds for memory entries. 0 = session-scoped (cleared when connection closes). Omitting means no expiry — increases PII exposure risk (MEM-02).

connection.description: Connection string for redis or sqlite backends. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

connectionString.description: Database connection URI. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

table.description: Table or collection name where agent memories are stored. Defaults to "agent_memories" if omitted. Use a per-agent name to avoid cross-agent data leakage.

ttlDays.description: Time-to-live in days for long-term memory records. Omitting means no expiry — a compliance risk (MEM-02). Required by GDPR Article 5 data minimisation principle.

connectionString.description: Connection URI for self-hosted backends (pgvector, weaviate, qdrant, chroma). Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

apiKey.description: API key for managed backends (Pinecone, Weaviate Cloud). Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

dimension.description: Embedding vector dimension. Must exactly match the output dimension of your embedding model (e.g. 1536 for text-embedding-3-small, 768 for nomic-embed-text). Mismatch causes insertion errors.

topK.description: Number of nearest-neighbour results to retrieve per similarity search. Higher values improve recall at the cost of latency and context token usage.

namespace.description: Logical partition within the vector store. Required by MEM-04 to prevent cross-agent data leakage. Recommended value: the agent name (spec.metadata.name).

piiScrubFields.description: PII field names to scrub before writing to long-term or vector memory. Required when longTerm is set (MEM-01). Use recogniser names compatible with your scrubbing library (e.g. Microsoft Presidio entity types): "email", "phone_number", "credit_card", "iban", "ssn", "bank_account", "passport", "drivers_license", "person_name", "date_of_birth", "address", "ip_address", "api_key", "password", "medical_record".

auditLog.description: When true, all memory read and write operations are emitted as structured audit log events. Required for long-term memory (MEM-03) and HIPAA/SOC2 compliance.

retentionPolicy.description: Named governance or compliance retention policy applied to this agent's memory. Informational label — must be consistent with ttlSeconds/ttlDays on the backend configs. Common values: "session" (cleared on disconnect), "ephemeral" (never persisted), "30d", "90d", "1y", "persistent" (explicit no-expiry opt-in), "gdpr-30d" (GDPR Art.5 minimum), "hipaa-6y" (HIPAA 45 CFR §164.530), "pci-dss-1y" (PCI DSS Req.10.7).

header.description: HTTP header name for the API key (e.g. "X-API-Key"). Only used when auth type is "apikey".

invocation.description: "parallel" = invoked concurrently with other subagents, "sequential" = invoked in order after previous subagents complete, "on-demand" = only invoked when the agent decides it is needed (triggered by LLM or triggerKeywords).

passContext.description: When true, the current conversation thread (messages so far) is forwarded to the subagent as context. Use with care — increases token usage and may leak sensitive conversation history.

triggerKeywords.description: Keywords in user messages that automatically trigger this subagent (only applies when invocation is "on-demand"). Case-insensitive substring match.

pathPrefix.description: URL path prefix applied to all API routes (e.g. "/api/v1"). Useful when the agent runs behind a reverse proxy.

jwksUri.description: JWKS endpoint URL used to fetch public keys for JWT signature verification (e.g. "https://your-auth.example.com/.well-known/jwks.json"). Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

header.description: HTTP header name carrying the credential (e.g. "Authorization" for JWT bearer, "X-API-Key" for apikey auth). Defaults to "Authorization".

requestsPerMinute.description: Maximum requests allowed per client per minute. Excess requests receive HTTP 429.

requestsPerHour.description: Maximum requests allowed per client per hour. Excess requests receive HTTP 429.

streaming.description: When true, the API supports streaming responses (SSE). Set true for conversational agents to reduce perceived latency.

healthEndpoint.description: URL path for the health check endpoint (e.g. "/health", "/ready"). Exposes agentspec health report. Defaults to "/agentspec/health" when using the SDK.

metricsEndpoint.description: URL path where Prometheus-format metrics are exposed (e.g. "/metrics"). Required for observability.metrics to be scraped.

corsOrigins.description: Allowed CORS origins for browser clients (e.g. ["https://app.example.com", "http://localhost:3000"]). Use ["*"] only for fully public APIs.

path.description: URL path for the chat endpoint (e.g. "/v1/chat", "/api/chat"). Must start with "/".

protocol.description: "openai-compatible" = request/response shape matches the OpenAI Chat Completions API (enables drop-in compatibility with OpenAI SDKs). "custom" = proprietary shape defined by the agent.

streaming.description: When true, the endpoint supports Server-Sent Events streaming responses. Clients receive tokens as they are generated.

sessionMode.description: "stateful" = conversation history is persisted per thread (requires threadIdHeader). "stateless" = each request is independent; no memory across calls.

threadIdHeader.description: HTTP request header used to identify the conversation thread for stateful session routing (e.g. "X-Thread-Id", "X-Session-Id").

id.description: Skill identifier as registered in the runtime skill registry (e.g. "code-interpreter", "image-gen").

version.description: Pinned skill version. Omit to use the latest available version. Semver range or exact version string.

blockedTopics.description: Topics or keywords that should be blocked in user input (e.g. ["competitor-names", "legal-advice"]). Case-insensitive substring match against the full message.

action.description: "reject" = refuse the request and return an error, "warn" = continue but emit a warning event, "log" = silently log the violation.

message.description: Custom error message returned to the user when a topic is blocked. Defaults to a generic refusal message.

action.description: "scrub" = replace detected PII with placeholders before passing to the model, "reject" = refuse the request, "warn" = pass through but emit a warning event.

fields.description: Specific PII entity types to detect. When omitted, all PII types supported by the detection library are scanned. Use the same entity names as in spec.memory.hygiene.piiScrubFields.

sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity. "low" = only block obvious injection patterns, "medium" = balanced (default), "high" = aggressive — may produce false positives on legitimate complex instructions.

module.description: Path to the module implementing the guardrail function. Use "$file:guardrails/my_check.py" syntax.

function.description: Name of the guardrail function exported from the module. Signature: (input: str) -> None (raise to block) or (input: str) -> str (return scrubbed value).

input.description: Guardrails applied to user input before it is sent to the model. Evaluated in order — first blocking rule wins.

threshold.description: Confidence score below which a response is considered hallucinated (0.0–1.0). 0.0 = block everything, 1.0 = block nothing. Typical value: 0.5–0.7.

action.description: "reject" = return error to user, "retry" = re-invoke the model (up to maxRetries), "warn" = return response with a warning annotation.

maxRetries.description: Maximum re-generation attempts before giving up. Only used when action is "retry". Defaults to 1.

threshold.description: Toxicity score above which a response is blocked (0.0–1.0). 0.0 = block all output, 1.0 = block nothing. Typical production value: 0.7–0.85.

action.description: "scrub" = replace PII in the model output before returning to the user, "reject" = discard the response and return an error, "warn" = return with a warning annotation.

fields.description: Specific PII entity types to detect in model output. Omit to scan all types. Use the same entity names as in spec.memory.hygiene.piiScrubFields.

module.description: Path to the module implementing the output guardrail. Use "$file:guardrails/output_check.py" syntax.

function.description: Name of the guardrail function. Signature: (output: str) -> None (raise to block) or (output: str) -> str (return transformed value).

output.description: Guardrails applied to model output before it is returned to the user. Evaluated in order — first blocking rule wins.

approvalRequired.description: Conditions that pause execution and request human approval. "before-destructive-tool" = any tool with destructiveHint=true, "on-low-confidence" = model confidence below a threshold, "on-high-cost" = single call exceeds cost controls.

timeoutSeconds.description: Seconds to wait for human approval before executing the timeoutAction. Default 300s (5 minutes). Max 3600s (1 hour).

timeoutAction.description: "reject" = abort the action and return an error (default, safest), "proceed" = continue without approval (use only for low-risk actions), "escalate" = forward to a higher-level approver or on-call channel.

notifyVia.description: Channels used to notify humans when approval is required. Multiple channels can be specified; all are notified simultaneously.

webhookUrl.description: URL to POST approval requests to when notifyVia includes "webhook". The payload contains the pending action and a callback token. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

name.description: Unique dataset identifier, referenced by "agentspec evaluate --dataset <name>".

path.description:
Path to the evaluation dataset file (JSONL format). Use "$file:evals/my_dataset.jsonl" syntax. Each line is a JSON object with the following fields:
  input            (required) – user query sent to the agent.
  expected         (required) – expected output; used for string_match and answer_similarity scoring.
  context          (optional) – string[] of retrieved chunks the agent actually used. Required for faithfulness, context_precision, hallucination metrics.
  reference_contexts (optional) – string[] of ground-truth relevant chunks. Required for context_recall metric.
  tags             (optional) – string[] labels for filtering with --tag.
  metadata         (optional) – arbitrary key/value pairs for reporting (e.g. {"difficulty": "hard"}).
Example: {"input":"What is RAG?","expected":"Retrieval Augmented Generation","context":["RAG combines retrieval..."],"tags":["rag"]}

metrics.description: Metrics to compute for this specific dataset. Overrides (narrows) spec.evaluation.metrics for this dataset only. When omitted, the global spec.evaluation.metrics list is used. Example: a RAG dataset sets ["faithfulness","context_recall"] while a safety dataset sets ["toxicity","bias"].

datasets.description: Evaluation datasets. Each entry maps a name to a JSONL file and optionally scopes which metrics to compute.

metrics.description: Default metrics to compute across all datasets that do not declare their own metrics. "faithfulness", "context_precision", "context_recall" require the "context" field in dataset samples. "context_recall" additionally requires "reference_contexts". "custom" requires a scorer registered in the evaluation framework config.

thresholds.description: Pass/fail thresholds per metric (0.0–1.0). Key = metric name, value = minimum required score. Example: {"faithfulness": 0.8, "answer_relevancy": 0.75}. Used by ciGate and "agentspec evaluate".

ciGate.description: When true, "agentspec evaluate" exits with code 1 if any metric falls below its threshold. Blocks CI pipelines on evaluation regression (EVAL-01).

endpoint.description: Tracing collector endpoint URL. Required for "otel", "honeycomb", and self-hosted backends. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

publicKey.description: Public/project API key for the tracing backend (e.g. Langfuse public key). Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

secretKey.description: Secret API key for the tracing backend (e.g. Langfuse secret key). Always use "$secret:" or "$env:" — never hardcode. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

sampleRate.description: Fraction of requests to trace (0.0–1.0). 1.0 = trace every request (default), 0.1 = trace 10% for high-traffic cost savings. Set to 1.0 during development.

endpoint.description: Metrics export endpoint (e.g. OTLP endpoint for OpenTelemetry, Datadog agent URL). Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

serviceName.description: Service name used to label all emitted metrics and traces. Defaults to metadata.name. Use a consistent name across deployments for accurate dashboards.

structured.description: When true, logs are emitted as JSON objects (structured logging). Required for log aggregation platforms (Datadog, Splunk, CloudWatch). Set false only for local development readability.

redactFields.description: Field names to redact from structured log output before writing to log sinks. Use the same values as spec.memory.hygiene.piiScrubFields plus any application-specific sensitive keys (e.g. ["password", "credit_card", "session_token"]).

packs.description: Compliance rule packs to evaluate during "agentspec audit". Each pack groups related rules. Omit to run all packs.

rule.description: Rule ID to suppress (e.g. "SEC-LLM-03", "MEM-02"). Must match an existing compliance rule ID.

reason.description: Human-readable justification for suppressing this rule. Required for audit trails. Example: "Hallucination detection handled by external Lakera Guard proxy."

approvedBy.description: Name or identifier of the person who approved this suppression (e.g. "security-team", "alice@example.com"). Used for compliance sign-off tracking.

expires.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this suppression expires and should be re-evaluated (e.g. "2025-12-31"). Suppressions without an expiry are flagged in audits.

suppressions.description: Rules to suppress in audit output. Each suppression requires a reason and optional expiry date. Over-suppression is itself flagged.

auditSchedule.description: How often the compliance audit should be re-run in CI. "on-change" = re-audit on every manifest change (recommended). Informational — enforcement depends on your CI configuration.

envVars.description: Environment variable names that must be set at agent startup. The SDK validates these at load time and throws a descriptive error if any are missing (e.g. ["OPENAI_API_KEY", "DATABASE_URL"]).

connection.description: Connection URI for this service. Supports reference syntax: "$env:VAR_NAME" (env var), "$secret:name" (secret manager), "$file:path" (file relative to agent.yaml), or a literal string value.

services.description: External services that must be reachable at startup. The SDK health check verifies TCP connectivity to each service.

minimumMemoryMB.description: Minimum RAM required to run the agent in megabytes (e.g. 512, 2048). Used by orchestrators for resource scheduling. Does not enforce a limit — purely declarative.

nodeVersion.description: Minimum Node.js version required, as a semver range or exact version (e.g. ">=20.0.0", "20.x"). Used by CI and orchestrators for compatibility checks.

pythonVersion.description: Minimum Python version required, as a semver range or exact version (e.g. ">=3.11", "3.12"). Used by CI and orchestrators for compatibility checks.
